questions.question: What is the evidence grade for family therapy effectiveness?

options.text: Strong for specific conditions (adolescent substance use, eating disorders, family conflict); Moderate for general functioning

questions.explanation: Evidence grade is STRONG for specific conditions including adolescent substance use, eating disorders, and family conflict, with systematic reviews confirming superior outcomes to individual therapy alone. Evidence is MODERATE for general family functioning. The 2024 Lancet meta-analysis demonstrated multi-component interventions outperform single-focus approaches.

questions.question: Which family therapy approach is the gold standard for adolescent eating disorders?
questions.explanation: Family-Based Treatment (FBT), also called the Maudsley Approach, is the gold standard for adolescent anorexia nervosa with strong evidence for faster weight restoration and recovery compared to individual therapy. Parents take temporary control of eating with therapist support. This requires specialized training and positions parents as essential allies in recovery.

questions.question: What are the three phases of typical family therapy?

options.text: Assessment (1-3 sessions), Active Treatment (8-20 sessions), Termination and Maintenance

questions.explanation: Family therapy typically involves: Assessment Phase (1-3 sessions gathering history, observing interactions, providing feedback), Active Treatment (8-20 sessions changing patterns, building skills), and Termination/Maintenance (achieving goals, scheduling check-ins, planning for future). Progress is monitored throughout and approach adjusted based on what's working.

questions.question: When finding a family therapist, which credential specifically indicates training in family therapy?

options.text: Any licensed professional can do family therapy

questions.explanation: LMFT (Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist) specifically indicates training in family therapy. LCSW (social worker) or LPC (counselor) with family therapy training, or psychologists with family specialization can also provide family therapy, but LMFT is the credential specifically designed for family therapy practice. Always verify licensing and ask about specialized training for your specific concern.

questions.question: Which situations require immediate professional family therapy rather than self-guided support?

options.text: Family violence, child suicidal thoughts, eating disorders, substance abuse, severe trauma, complete communication breakdown
Only when all family members agree they need help

questions.explanation: Immediate professional help is required for: family violence/abuse, child/adolescent expressing suicidal thoughts or self-harm, eating disorder behaviors, substance abuse affecting family, trauma affecting multiple members, severe parental conflict damaging children, complete communication breakdown lasting months, or anyone feeling unsafe in the home. For safety emergencies, call 988, go to ER, or contact local crisis services immediately.

title: Family Therapy and When to Seek Professional Help - Quiz
